lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- The Full Interview & Program Review
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Pelizaro | Aula 16 -- The Full Interview

listenings.text: Good afternoon. Thank you for the final conversation. Let me walk you through one project from start to finish, the way you asked. The situation was a release that was eight weeks late when I joined. The task was clear: get it back on track without cutting quality. So here is what I did. First, I rebuilt the plan and I cut the scope to the features that really mattered. Then I aligned the stakeholders and I set a weekly checkpoint with the whole team. The result was strong. We shipped the product on time, and the client renewed the contract for two more years. I rehearsed this story many times, but the numbers are real. My mindset is simple: stay calm, communicate clearly, and own the outcome. I am happy to go deeper into any decision I made along the way.

Hi Rafael, this is Sofia again, with great news. The engineering director loved the walkthrough of your project. He said your composure was excellent and that you articulate complex ideas in a simple way. After a short debrief with the panel, we have made a decision. We would like to offer you the senior project manager role. Congratulations. A few next steps: I will send the formal offer by email this afternoon, with the salary, the start date, and the benefits. Please read it carefully and reply within five business days. If you have any questions, just call me. One more thing from the whole team. You have grown a lot since your first interview. You showcase your track record with confidence now, and you handle hard questions with a calm mindset. Welcome aboard, and talk very soon.

extra_audio.text: First, set the situation: where were you and what was the context? Next, explain the task: what was your goal or the problem to solve? Then describe the action: what did you actually do, step by step? After that, share the result with a real number, like on time or two years. Finally, add one short takeaway: what did you learn and how do you use it now?
